title: campanula garganica subsp. garganica
intro: Campanula garganica subsp. garganica, also known as Garganic Bellflower, is a species of flowering plant in the family Campanulaceae, characterized by its bell-shaped flowers and compact, spreading habit. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, and its medicinal properties are attributed to its high content of saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Campanula garganica subsp. garganica include its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may help to soothe digestive issues, reduce stress, and protect against cell damage. Campanula garganica subsp. garganica can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional herbal medicine as a topical application to treat skin irritations and wounds.
